_note: EP-01 pilot v3 — HAND-WRITTEN master pass (Dwij + Claude, 2026-07-15). ~5:00 cut: incident by 1:35, loop diagram drawn, two choices shown, human outcome stated. The pipeline may not rewrite a word of it.
topic: The Underground City That Cooked Itself
title: The Underground City That Cooked Itself
title_options: The Underground City That Cooked Itself
This City's Life Support Turned On It
How One Aquifer Killed a City of 9,400
The Machine That Farmed Its Own Killer
Sealed From the Outside, In Good Order
thumb_headline: Its own life support turned on it
thumb_prompt: a massive circular basalt hatch set into a cracked desert plateau at dusk, heat shimmer rising off the stone, a faint rust-black crust creeping from the hatch seams, one small human figure in dusty field gear standing before it, warm dim amber K-dwarf sunlight, low angle, ochre dust haze, real depth in the background, documentary photograph, 35mm, film grain, bottom third of frame kept clear

thumb_options.concept: the sealed hatch with heat shimmer and creeping black crust, tiny human for scale
a single massive pipe descending into darkness, rust-black film blooming on its flank
a hand pressing a seal stamp onto hatch documentation, city lights dying below

settlement.name: Tehlmark Deep
settlement.premise: A city cut vertically into basalt to escape a surface that cooks — eleven levels, the deepest 340 metres down.

systems.name: COOLING
systems.how: A closed water loop gathers waste heat from coil banks on every level and rejects it into an aquifer 500 metres below the lowest gallery.

systems.name: WATER
systems.how: The drinking supply, drawn up, filtered, arriving pre-chilled by the deep rock.

systems.name: POWER
systems.how: Geothermal taps sunk into the same hot basalt the city hides from.

systems.name: AIR
systems.how: Passive stack ventilation — the day-night temperature swing at the surface pumps the shafts like a slow lung.

systems.name: FOOD
systems.how: Fungal galleries on level 9, fed on organic fractions sieved from surface dust.

hidden_dependency: Cooling and drinking water both terminate in one aquifer — a coupling nobody ever drew on the same diagram.

stress_event.name: The Bloom
stress_event.trigger: During dust season, a surface intake filter change went eleven days overdue — the replacement crew was repairing a crane.
cascade: Biologically active volcanic dust passes the tired filters and settles on the warm, wet cooling coils of every level
Cinder film colonises the coil surfaces and halves their thermal conductivity within 60 days
Pumps run harder to compensate; pump waste heat enters the same loop it is trying to empty
The aquifer, warmed by sixty years of rejected heat plus the new load, crosses 45 C — the film's optimal growth band — and growth accelerates
Cooling capacity falls below the geothermal baseline; deep-gallery air temperature rises roughly one degree per week
Drinking water reaches 46 C on day 211; evacuation ordered on day 214

verdict_reason: Two systems shared one aquifer, on a page nobody drew.
real_world_basis: Industrial biofouling of heat exchangers — a real, multi-billion-dollar engineering problem in cooling systems worldwide
Iron- and sulphur-metabolising extremophile biofilms (Acidithiobacillus, Sulfolobus; the Rio Tinto river ecosystem)
Thermal breakthrough in geothermal doublets — reinjected waste heat gradually warming the source aquifer
Closed-loop district cooling systems, where pump work itself becomes part of the heat load
Deferred-maintenance failure cascades, where a trivial scheduling slip compounds into system loss
premise: A buried city ran flawlessly for sixty-one years on five systems — two of which shared a resource nobody drew on the same diagram.
hero_prompt: the heat-exchanger gallery of an underground city — a long basalt vault lined with pipe manifolds and finned coil banks, warm amber worklights, thin haze, a single technician with a hand lamp for scale, documentary photograph, 35mm, film grain, matte contrast
forbidden_visuals: a creature with a face or eyes
next_tease_topic: The Convoy That Outran the Sun
description: Original speculative documentary. The place is fictional; the principles are real. Tehlmark Deep buried itself to survive a desert that cooks — then its own life support, fed by a maintenance slip and a mineral-eating film, turned the city into an oven it could not switch off. Case one of the Kelvara survey files. #speculativefiction #worldbuilding #documentary

scenes.title: A city with nobody in it
scenes.narration: Three hundred and forty metres beneath the worst desert on this world, there is a city with nobody in it. Nothing broke in. Nothing exploded. The machine that kept nine and a half thousand people alive is what cooked them out — and every repair its engineers tried made the heat worse. The place is fictional. The principles are real. The galleries held thirty-one degrees for sixty-one years. Remember that number.
must_show: a sealed circular hatch in desert rock
scenes.ai_prompt: a massive circular basalt hatch sealed into a cracked desert plateau, heat shimmer rising, fine ochre dust drifting past, dim warm amber late-afternoon sunlight, no people, documentary photograph, 35mm, slight film grain

scenes.title: The Cinder Shelf
scenes.narration: This is Kelvara — thin air, a thirty-one-hour day, and heat that does not want to leave. And this is the Cinder Shelf, a desert where the afternoon alone can kill. Nothing lives on the surface here. So the people went under it.
must_show: the atlas locator on the Cinder Shelf

scenes.title: Tehlmark Deep
scenes.narration: Tehlmark Deep. Eleven floors cut straight down; nine thousand four hundred people. Miners first, then families, then children who never once saw the surface at noon. Fungus farms for food. Hot rock below for power. Schools ran, and the night shift walked home under strings of amber lights. An ordinary town, in an extraordinary hole. Thirty-one degrees, every single year.
must_show: a vast vertical shaft city interior
scenes.ai_prompt: interior of a vast vertical city shaft carved through black basalt, ringed galleries descending into warm darkness, strings of amber worklights, railed walkways with small human figures, thin haze, documentary photograph, 35mm, film grain

scenes.title: Five systems, one page
scenes.narration: Here is the whole city on one page. Power from the hot rock. Air pumped by the day-night swing. Food from the farms. Cooling — a loop of water that collects heat from every floor and dumps it into a cold underground lake, an aquifer, five hundred metres further down. And drinking water, drawn from that same lake. Look once more. Two of those lines end in the same place. Nobody thought it mattered. An audit called the design elegant. It was.
must_show: two lines meeting one aquifer node

schematic.headline: Five systems, one page
schematic.body: Cooling rejects into the aquifer. The taps draw from it.
schematic.label: CORE TEMP

scenes.title: Eleven days
scenes.narration: Year sixty-one, dust season. The air-shaft filters were due for a change every forty days. The crew was stuck fixing a crane — the filters ran eleven days over. That is the entire trigger. Storm dust slipped down the shafts and settled on the cooling loop's warm, wet pipes on every floor. Most of it was rock. Some of it was alive: a crust that eats iron and sulphur, and grows fastest at exactly the temperatures inside a cooling pipe. Field crews named it cinder film. Thirty-three.
must_show: dust pouring down a ventilation shaft
scenes.ai_prompt: extreme close-up of a matte rust-black biofilm crust spreading across a finned metal cooling coil, faint oily iridescent bloom along its growing edge, beads of condensation, warm amber worklight, macro documentary photograph, shallow depth of field, film grain

scenes.title: A blanket half a millimetre thick
scenes.narration: A cooling pipe works by letting heat cross its wall. The film is a blanket, half a millimetre thick, laid over every pipe the city owned. In sixty days, half the cooling was gone — and engineers on our own world fight this exact enemy in cooling towers. They call it biofouling. Thirty-seven.

scenes.title: The trap
scenes.narration: Now watch the trap close. The pumps ran harder to push more water — but pumps are motors, and motors make heat. The lake had swallowed sixty years of the city's warmth; it was no longer cold. Warmer water grows the film faster. More film, less cooling. Less cooling, harder pumping. Harder pumping, warmer water. Around the circle, each turn feeding the next. The city was not fighting the film any more. It was farming it. Forty-one.
must_show: the feedback loop drawn as a circle

scenes.title: Two moves left
scenes.narration: That left two moves, and both lost. Option one: shut the loop down and starve the film. But this air is thin — a sealed city does not cool, it soaks. Fifty degrees in the nurseries within two days. Option two: keep pumping, and feed the thing that is killing you. They kept pumping. On day two hundred and eleven, the drinking water came out of the taps at forty-six degrees. Forty-six.

options.title: Shut the loop down
options.consequence: A sealed city soaks — 50 C in the nurseries within two days

options.title: Keep pumping
options.consequence: Every watt becomes heat — the lake warms, the film feeds

scenes.title: The walk-out
scenes.narration: Day two hundred and fourteen. The council ordered the walk-out. Nine thousand four hundred people climbed eleven floors into the night — carrying children, tools, and the city's paper records. The last crew out sealed the doors behind them, carefully, in good order. You do not leave a door open on a world like this. Forty-six.
must_show: a long line of people climbing at night
scenes.ai_prompt: a long orderly line of people in dusty field clothes climbing a vast spiral stairway cut into black basalt at night, carrying bags and children, lit by strings of amber emergency lights, haze below them, seen from above, documentary photograph, 35mm, film grain

scenes.title: Verdict
scenes.narration: Every one of the nine thousand four hundred walked out alive. The people adapted. The city did not. Tehlmark Deep held for sixty-one years and fell in two hundred and fourteen days — because two systems shared one lake, on a page nobody drew. Verdict: failed. Fifty-one degrees when the last sensor died.

card.headline: FAILED
card.body: Two systems shared one lake, on a page nobody drew.

scenes.title: The survey moves on
scenes.narration: The survey moved east, to where the Shelf steps down into a slow, warm sea whose tide arrives before its moon does. That file stays closed. The next case never buried itself at all: a convoy three kilometres long that survives the afternoon by outrunning it — and has been driving for ninety years.
must_show: a coastline at dusk from high altitude
scenes.ai_prompt: aerial view at dusk of a dark basalt plateau descending in giant steps to a warm shallow sea, faint bioluminescent shimmer far out on the water, dim amber horizon light, ochre haze, documentary photograph from high altitude, 35mm, film grain
